studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The primary endpoint is assessed at enrollment, after 3 weeks, 1 day pre-surgery, 2 weeks post-surgery, 6 weeks post-surgery, 10 weeks post-surgery, and 14 weeks post-surgery.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group: Standard of care + Use of the Orthopy app. Control group: Standard of care.
studies.collected_data: Primary outcome: KOOS (knee injury and osteoarthritis outcome score). Secondary outcomes: TAMPA scale (kinesiophobia), numeric VAS (subjective pain level, subjective function, subjective patient satisfaction), physical therapy/lymphatic drainage needed (yes/no), ability to work (yes/no), patient-relevant structural and procedural improvements (pSVV), thigh circumference, lower leg circumference, patellar center, angular measurements (range of motion), stability test (anteroposterior stability of the knee joint), coordination, quick strength, agility (end of study only), exploratory endpoints on app use (intervention group only) via custom-developed questions.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: The clinical trial showed positive outcomes on the KOOS Pain and Symptoms subscale in the intervention group compared to the standard of care group. Statistically significant pain reduction was observed at t1 (p = 0.0005), t2 (p = 0.0068), and t4 (approximately 6 weeks post-surgery; p = 0.01). Significant symptom reduction was noted at t1 (p = 0.0001), t2 (p = 0.0036), and t6 (p = 0.0377). The KOOS subscales for quality of life (p = 0.0066), activities of daily living (p = 0.0009), and sport (p = 0.0484) showed significant effects before surgery.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The intervention period includes a preoperative phase of at least 1 week and a postoperative phase of 14 weeks.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group: Postoperative use of the Orthopy app in addition to usual care. Control group: Usual care consisting of routine medical treatment and physiotherapy prescriptions.
studies.collected_data: VAS Pain (primary outcome) assessed at t0, t1, t2, t3, t4, t5, t6. Secondary outcomes include WOMET, KOOS subscales (pain, symptoms, function), angle reproduction test, and range of motion (ROM). Data collected digitally via online questionnaires and during physical visits at study centers.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The intervention period lasts at least 18 weeks, including a preoperative phase of at least 4 weeks and a postoperative phase of 14 weeks.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group: Use of the Orthopy app in addition to usual care. Control group: Usual care alone.
studies.collected_data: Primary endpoint: KOOS Symptoms subscale. Secondary endpoints: KOOS-ACL Function, VAS Pain, LERAS-13, KOOS QoL, Angle Reproduction Test. Data collected at T0 (28-36 days pre-op), T1 (1 day pre-op), T2 (3 days post-op), T3 (2 weeks post-op), T4 (6 weeks post-op), T5 (10 weeks post-op), T6 (14 weeks post-op).

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The intervention period consists of a preoperative phase of at least 2 weeks and a postoperative phase of 14 weeks, totaling at least 16 weeks.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group: Pre- and postoperative use of the Orthopy app in addition to standard orthopedic therapy. Control group: Standard therapy in Germany (regular physiotherapy).
studies.collected_data: Primary Outcome: KOOS (knee injury and osteoarthritis outcome score) Subscale Symptoms. Secondary Outcomes: KOOS Pain subscale, Subjective pain level (numerical VAS), Subjective function (numerical VAS), KOOS Activities of Daily Living subscale, KOOS Quality of Life subscale, KOOS Sport subscale. Exploratory endpoints: Thigh circumference, range of motion (extension, neutral position, and flexion via angle measure).

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Each participant is involved in the study for about 12 weeks, which corresponds to the usual follow-up period after ACL surgery.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Group 1: Combination therapy (shockwave therapy and cryotherapy); Group 2: Shockwave therapy only; Group 3: Cryotherapy only; Group 4: Control group with standardized physiotherapy only.
studies.collected_data: IKDC score at 12 weeks postoperatively (primary outcome); Tegner activity scores, Lysholm scores, VAS pain scores, swelling status, and range of motion at 1, 3, 6, and 12 weeks postoperatively; IKDC score at 1, 3, and 6 weeks postoperatively; physiotherapy-related data collected via the Orthopy app.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The study included a presurgery period (2-6 weeks) and a postsurgery period (14 weeks). Interim assessments were done at multiple time points, including t1 (if more than 3 weeks before surgery), t3, t4, t5 (postsurgery), and t6 (14 weeks postsurgery).
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: The intervention group received app-assisted therapy plus standard of care, while the control group received only the standard of care.
studies.collected_data: KOOS (Knee injury and Osteoarthritis Outcome Score) as the primary outcome measure. Secondary endpoints included subjective pain and function levels measured by the numeric rating scale (NRS). Baseline data (t0) and interim assessments were conducted at t1 (if more than 3 weeks before surgery), t2 (presurgery), t3, t4, t5 (postsurgery), and t6 (14 weeks postsurgery).
studies.key_outcomes_findings: The intervention group showed significant improvements in the KOOS Pain and Symptoms subscale compared to the control group. Statistically significant pain reduction was observed at t1 (p = 0.0005), t2 (p = 0.0068), and t4 (approximately 6 weeks after surgery; p = 0.01). Significant symptom reduction was noted at t1 (p = 0.0001), t2 (p = 0.0036), and t6 (p = 0.0377). The KOOS subscales for quality of life (p = 0.0066), activities of daily living (p = 0.0009), and sport (p = 0.0484) showed significant effects before surgery. The pain NRS showed significant benefits at t4 and positive tendencies at t3, t5, and t6.

audit.study_title: Use of a smartphone application (Orthopy App) to improve preoperative and postoperative therapy in patients with ACL rupture compared to conventional standard of care treatment - a randomized, monocentric study.

Investigation of the effectiveness of integrated care using the Orthopy app in addition to usual care compared to usual care alone in patients with meniscal lesions – a randomized, controlled, multicenter trial
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results

audit.study_title: Investigation of the effectiveness of integrated care using the Orthopy app in addition to usual care compared to usual care alone in patients with anterior cruciate ligament rupture (with and without meniscal lesions) – a randomized, controlled, multicenter trial
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results

Use of a smartphone application (Orthopy App) to improve pre- and
postoperative therapy in patients after anterior cruciate ligament rupture
(with or without meniscal damage) or meniscal damage alone compared
to conventional standard of care treatment - a randomized, controlled,
multicenter study

audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results

audit.study_title: Combining a digital health application with standard care significantly enhances rehabilitation outcomes for ACL surgery patients.
